surface: Playwright (DOM-level deterministic browser control via @playwright/mcp attached to a real, Chrome-Sync'd Chrome over CDP)
recommended_approach: Choose option (b): keep @playwright/mcp as the MCP surface, but reconfigure it to ATTACH to a separately-launched real Chrome via --cdp-endpoint http://127.0.0.1:9222 instead of letting it launch its own bundled Chromium. The dedicated synced profile is launched by a small wrapper (a PowerShell/cmd launcher the /computer-control skill owns) using the real Chrome.exe with --remote-debugging-port=9222 --user-data-dir=<dedicated profile>. This is the only path that satisfies all four FIXED DECISIONS simultaneously: (1) it drives Alton's REAL synced Chrome, so Chrome's own password manager autofill fires natively — Playwright suppresses the password-manager/autofill UI only when IT launches the browser (it injects --enable-automation + credentials_enable_service=false prefs); when you connectOverCDP to a Chrome you launched yourself with normal flags, that Chrome behaves exactly as it does for a human, so saved-password autofill works. (2) A dedicated --user-data-dir is mandatory anyway under the Chrome 136+ security change (remote-debugging is refused against the default profile dir; a non-default dir is explicitly allowed), and a distinct user-data-dir means a distinct SingletonLock, so this Chrome runs concurrently with Alton's daily Chrome without the singleton-join problem. (3) @playwright/mcp gives deterministic DOM-selector tools (browser_click/type/fill_form/snapshot/select_option) AND, with --caps vision, coordinate tools for canvas/PDF/non-DOM cases. (4) The SAME --remote-debugging-port=9222 endpoint is simultaneously attachable by the claude-in-chrome extension bridge and the existing tools/chrome-tools cdp-*.ps1 toolkit, so one launched Chrome serves all three browser surfaces. Rejected (a) — pointing @playwright/mcp's own launch at the synced dir — because @playwright/mcp launching Chromium itself (i) holds the SingletonLock so a second human/extension attach is fragile, (ii) applies the automation prefs that suppress native password autofill, defeating the Chrome-password-first requirement, and (iii) historically still grabs bundled Chromium in some versions even when --user-data-dir is set (issue #921). Rejected (c) — a standalone connectOverCDP Node/Python script — as the PRIMARY surface because it duplicates everything @playwright/mcp already exposes as first-class agent tools and loses the snapshot/auto-wait ergonomics; keep (c) only as an escape hatch for multi-context/file-download flows the MCP cannot express.
setup_steps: Pick the dedicated profile path and port. Profile dir: C:\Users\alto8\chrome-computer-control-profile\ (NEW, distinct from the daily profile AND from the existing C:\Users\alto8\chrome-automation-profile used by chrome-tools on 9223). Port: 9222 for the synced/computer-control Chrome (keeps the legacy 9223 free; or standardize on 9223 if you want one port for everything — pick one and write it into the skill).

Create the launcher script the skill owns, e.g. C:\Users\alto8\Sartor-claude-network\tools\chrome-tools\launch-computer-control-chrome.ps1, body:  Start-Process "C:\Program Files (x86)\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe" -ArgumentList '--remote-debugging-port=9222','--remote-allow-origins=http://127.0.0.1:9222','--user-data-dir=C:\Users\alto8\chrome-computer-control-profile','--no-first-run','--no-default-browser-check','--restore-last-session'  (do NOT pass --headless; sync + password autofill need a headed, real profile).

ONE-TIME human onboarding: run the launcher, then in that Chrome window sign into Alton's Google account and turn Chrome Sync ON (Settings > You and Google > Turn on sync), confirm 'Passwords' is in the synced data types, and wait for the saved-password set to populate. This is a manual step Alton does once; the agent must not script Google login (Constitution-adjacent: no autonomous credential entry on Alton's account). After this, the profile dir persists logins/passwords across launches.

Verify the CDP endpoint is live and on the custom profile: open http://127.0.0.1:9222/json/version in any browser (or curl it) and confirm it returns a webSocketDebuggerUrl. If it returns nothing while Chrome is open, the launch joined an existing same-dir process — kill chrome processes for THAT profile only and relaunch (daily Chrome on its own dir is unaffected).

Reconfigure .mcp.json sartor-playwright: REPLACE the --user-data-dir/--viewport-size launch args with attach args. New args array: ["/c","npx","-y","@playwright/mcp@latest","--cdp-endpoint","http://127.0.0.1:9222","--caps","vision,pdf"]. Keep --caps vision,pdf (vision = the browser_mouse_*_xy coordinate tools already in the toolset; pdf = browser_pdf_save). Do NOT keep --browser chromium or --user-data-dir on the MCP line — under --cdp-endpoint the MCP attaches to the already-running Chrome and those flags are ignored/conflicting.

Restart the MCP client so the reconfigured sartor-playwright reconnects. On first browser_navigate, confirm the action lands in the EXISTING synced Chrome window (you'll see the real profile's tabs/avatar), not a fresh Playwright Chromium. browser_snapshot should show the synced profile's page.

Wire startup ordering into the /computer-control skill: SKILL.md preflight = (1) check 127.0.0.1:9222/json/version; (2) if dead, run launch-computer-control-chrome.ps1 and poll the endpoint until it answers (use the Monitor/until-loop pattern, not a fixed sleep); (3) only then issue browser_* calls. Document that Chrome must be launched BEFORE the MCP tries to attach, since connectOverCDP attaches and never launches.

Credential fallback path: for sites with a saved Chrome password, just browser_navigate to the login page and let Chrome autofill (it fires because this is a real profile) — at most click the account chip / press the autofill suggestion. For sites NOT in Chrome's store, fall back to scripts/sartor-secret (bw CLI) to fetch the value and browser_type it into the field. Never log/print the retrieved value (secrets-via-bitwarden hygiene). Add a note that DPAPI decryption of 'Login Data' is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE per the fixed decision.

OS-control layer (full-desktop, 3 monitors) is SEPARATE from the browser layer and bolts on unchanged: reuse the existing tools/chrome-tools OS scripts (click-at.ps1, type-text.ps1, screenshot.ps1, screenshot-region.ps1 -Monitor 0|1|2|all, focus-chrome.ps1, capture-window.ps1). For richer desktop control add pyautogui (pip install pyautogui with C:\Python313\python.exe) — note pyautogui's screen coords are global across all 3 monitors, so screenshot-region/-Monitor is the way to map a target before a click. This is the OS half of /computer-control; Playwright owns the in-page half.

Document the teardown/restart playbook in SKILL.md: if the synced Chrome is closed, the MCP's CDP connection drops; the skill must relaunch Chrome and the MCP will re-attach on next call (or restart the MCP). Never close the synced Chrome out from under an in-flight task.
capabilities: Deterministic DOM-level control on the REAL synced profile: browser_click / browser_type / browser_fill_form / browser_select_option / browser_snapshot / browser_wait_for operate on accessibility-tree node refs with Playwright auto-waiting (element visible+stable+enabled before acting) — far more reliable than coordinate clicks and resilient to layout shift.

Native Chrome password-manager autofill works because the agent drives a real Chrome the agent did NOT launch via Playwright — saved/synced passwords prefill and can be accepted, satisfying the Chrome-password-first credential rule with zero secret handling.

Chrome Sync ON means any login/password Alton saves on any device propagates into this profile, so the agent inherits an authenticated browsing surface for Gmail, Calendar, vast.ai, Chase-adjacent portals, etc., without per-site token files.

Single launched Chrome on one CDP port is simultaneously usable by @playwright/mcp (DOM), the claude-in-chrome extension bridge, AND the legacy tools/chrome-tools cdp-*.ps1 toolkit — three browser surfaces, one browser process, one profile.
browser_pdf_save (pdf cap) for clean PDF capture, browser_network_requests for request inspection, browser_evaluate / browser_run_code_unsafe for arbitrary in-page JS when selectors aren't enough.
--caps vision adds browser_mouse_click_xy / move_xy / drag_xy / down / up / wheel for canvas, WebGL, PDF-viewer, drag-handles, and other non-DOM targets where there is no selectable element.
Concurrent with Alton's daily Chrome: distinct --user-data-dir = distinct SingletonLock = both browsers run side by side without interference.
limitations: connectOverCDP attaches only — it NEVER launches. Chrome MUST already be running with --remote-debugging-port before the MCP makes any call. If Chrome is closed or crashes, every browser_* tool fails until the launcher re-runs and the MCP re-attaches. Startup ordering is a hard dependency, not a nicety.

Chrome 136+ refuses --remote-debugging-port against the DEFAULT profile dir entirely. The dedicated --user-data-dir is therefore mandatory, not optional; you can never attach to Alton's actual daily profile this way (and shouldn't — it would also collide on the SingletonLock).

SingletonLock / process-join hazard: if the launcher's --user-data-dir ever overlaps a Chrome already open on that same dir, the new invocation silently joins the existing process and DOES NOT open the debug port. Must be the dedicated dir, and only one Chrome per dir.

connectOverCDP is documented as 'significantly lower fidelity than the Playwright protocol connection' — some advanced Playwright features (full tracing, certain download/route interception, multi-context isolation) are degraded vs a Playwright-launched browser. For those rare flows, use the standalone-script escape hatch (option c).

The CDP webSocketDebuggerUrl changes every Chrome restart, but @playwright/mcp's --cdp-endpoint points at the STABLE http://127.0.0.1:9222 (it resolves /json/version itself), so this only bites a raw connectOverCDP script that cached a ws:// URL — not the MCP. Still: pin the http endpoint, never a ws URL.

One-time Google sign-in + Sync-on is a manual human step. The agent must not automate Google account login. Until Alton does it once, the profile has no saved passwords and autofill has nothing to fire.

Coordinate (vision) clicks are brittle on a multi-monitor / DPI-scaled setup: browser_mouse_*_xy are viewport-relative to the attached page (1440x900-class viewport), while the OS-layer pyautogui/click-at.ps1 are GLOBAL desktop coords across 2560x1440 + 2 monitors. Don't mix the two coordinate spaces; map deliberately.

DPAPI decryption of Chrome 'Login Data' is out of scope by decision — so the agent cannot READ password values, only trigger Chrome to autofill them. Any site needing a typed credential not in Chrome must go through the Bitwarden fallback.
Full-desktop OS control (click/type anywhere) is high-blast: an errant global click can hit any app on any monitor. There is no DOM-style auto-wait or undo at the OS layer.
integration_notes: "This Playwright surface is the DOM-deterministic half of /computer-control; the OS layer (pyautogui + tools/chrome-tools OS scripts) is the pixel half. Router rule: if the target is an element inside a web page, use the Playwright DOM tools (browser_click/type/fill_form on snapshot refs) — they auto-wait and survive layout change. If the target is a canvas/PDF-viewer/drag-handle inside the page with no selectable node, use the --caps vision browser_mouse_*_xy tools. If the target is OUTSIDE the browser (any other Windows app, any of the 3 monitors), use the OS layer (pyautogui / click-at.ps1 with screenshot-region -Monitor to locate). All three browser surfaces (this MCP, the claude-in-chrome extension, the legacy cdp-*.ps1 toolkit) share ONE launched Chrome on 127.0.0.1:9222, so they coexist without extra browser processes — but they must agree on the port; standardize the skill on 9222 (or migrate the legacy 9223 toolkit to 9222) so there's a single endpoint. Concurrency with Alton's daily browsing is free because the dedicated profile dir gives an independent SingletonLock. The skill's preflight (launch-if-dead + poll-until-CDP-answers) is the seam that makes the attach model reliable — own it in SKILL.md."
security_flags: The dedicated profile is signed into Alton's REAL Google account with Sync ON, so it holds live authenticated sessions (Gmail, Calendar, Drive, vast.ai, financial portals). An agent with full DOM control of this profile can act AS Alton inside any of those sites. Constitution §7: no autonomous money movement, no sending under Alton's name without review — these now apply to browser actions, not just MCP/email tools. The skill must gate any send/submit/purchase/transfer click behind explicit confirmation, same as the email-draft rule.

An open --remote-debugging-port=9222 is a local-machine attack surface: ANY process on Rocinante can connect to that CDP endpoint and drive the authenticated browser or read its cookies (this is exactly the threat Chrome 136 hardened against for the default profile). Mitigate: bind/allow only 127.0.0.1 origins (--remote-allow-origins=http://127.0.0.1:9222), launch the debug Chrome only during active /computer-control sessions, and tear it down when idle rather than leaving an authenticated debug port open 24/7.

Children's-information and family-medical constraints (Constitution §7, family-calendar rule) extend to anything the agent reads/screenshots in this browser — e.g. school portals, medical sites — and to OS-layer screenshots of any monitor. Captured pixels/page text from such surfaces must not be logged to trajectories or externalized.

Bitwarden fallback handling: fetch via scripts/sartor-secret, type into the field, never echo to logs/console/output (secrets-via-bitwarden hygiene). The browser_evaluate / browser_run_code_unsafe tools can read page DOM including filled password fields — never serialize a filled credential field's value into tool output.

Full-desktop OS control means the agent can click/type into any app including Bitwarden's desktop UI, banking apps, or terminals — a mis-click is high-blast and irreversible. Treat OS-layer actions on sensitive apps as confirm-before-acting per the operational fail-safe.
open_questions: Port standardization: keep the legacy chrome-tools toolkit on 9223 and run computer-control Chrome on 9222 (two ports, two profiles, two Chromes), OR migrate everything to one port/one profile? One port is cleaner but means the synced-authenticated Chrome is the only automation Chrome — decide whether that's desirable or whether an unauthenticated scratch profile should stay separate.

Should the debug-port Chrome auto-launch at Rocinante login (always-on authenticated CDP surface, more convenient, larger attack window) or launch-on-demand per /computer-control session and tear down after (smaller window, slight latency)? The security flags favor on-demand.

Confirm @playwright/mcp@latest's current --cdp-endpoint behavior empirically once wired — past versions (issue #921) sometimes still launched bundled Chromium; verify the attach actually lands in the synced window on first run before trusting it for credentialed flows.

pyautogui is the assumed OS-control lib (pip install needed) — confirm that vs alternatives (e.g. the existing PowerShell click-at.ps1/type-text.ps1 may suffice for v1, deferring the pyautogui dependency).

surface: claude-in-chrome (Claude in Chrome browser extension, bridged to Claude Code via native-messaging named pipe)
recommended_approach: Adopt claude-in-chrome as the browser surface for the DEDICATED synced profile, and let it own that profile exclusively. It attaches to tabs via chrome.debugger.attach(tabId,"1.3"), runs inside the user's real, logged-in Chrome, opens its own visible tabs, and shares the live cookie/login state — which is exactly the FIXED requirement (synced profile + Chrome password autofill + concurrent with daily Chrome). Its `computer` tool is browser-viewport vision (screenshot + click-by-coordinate on the rendered page), so it is the right pick for messy/canvas pages, anti-CDP/anti-bot sites, accessibility-tree reads, and staying inside the authenticated session — none of which Playwright's separate clean-profile Chromium can do without re-login. The decisive architectural fact: Chrome allows only ONE debugger client per tab, and chrome.debugger (extension) and --remote-debugging-port (Playwright/CDP) cannot both own the same tab; Chrome 136+ also refuses --remote-debugging-port on the default profile. So do NOT try to point Playwright at the synced profile. Run TWO separate Chrome instances: the synced profile driven by the extension, and Playwright's existing isolated .playwright-profile Chromium. Each surface gets its own profile; there is no contention to resolve because they never share a browser process. OS-desktop reach (other monitors, address bar, non-Chrome apps) is explicitly out of claude-in-chrome's scope and must be served by the PowerShell/pyautogui OS layer of /computer-control.
setup_steps: Install the Claude in Chrome extension (Chrome Web Store id fcoeoabgfenejglbffodgkkbkcdhcgfn, v1.0.36+) into the DEDICATED synced profile only; sign that profile into Alton's Google account with Chrome Sync ON so saved passwords/logins flow in. Keep it isolated from his daily profile so both run concurrently.

Launch Claude Code with the synced profile's Chrome already running, then enable the bridge: run `/chrome` (or start with `claude --chrome`). Use `/chrome` to check connection status, reconnect, and — when more than one browser is connected — pick the synced-profile browser. Do NOT enable Chrome-by-default globally (it always-loads browser tools and inflates context per the official docs); load on demand for the /computer-control skill instead.

Confirm the native-messaging host registration on Windows at HKCU\Software\Google\Chrome\NativeMessagingHosts\com.anthropic.claude_code_browser_extension.json. If the extension isn't detected on first enable, restart Chrome (the host config is read at Chrome startup).

Before any tool call, ToolSearch-load the specific tools: `select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp,mcp__claude-in-chrome__computer,mcp__claude-in-chrome__navigate,mcp__claude-in-chrome__find,mcp__claude-in-chrome__read_page,mcp__claude-in-chrome__get_page_text,mcp__claude-in-chrome__javascript_tool` (plus form_input, tabs_create_mcp, read_console_messages, read_network_requests, gif_creator, file_upload as the task needs). Schemas are deferred and uninvocable until loaded.

First operational call in any session is tabs_context_mcp (createIfEmpty:true) to obtain the live tabGroupId + tabId. Treat tab-ids as session-scoped: re-fetch context each session, never reuse a tab-id captured in a prior session.
Manage which sites the agent may act on via the Chrome EXTENSION's own site-permission settings (Claude Code inherits these). Set the permission posture there before relying on autonomous runs.

Set the router policy in /computer-control: claude-in-chrome = authenticated/anti-bot/vision-on-messy-page browser work in the synced profile; sartor-playwright (existing .playwright-profile) = deterministic DOM automation, PDF save, throwaway/clean-session scraping; PowerShell+pyautogui OS layer = anything outside the browser viewport (other monitors, OS dialogs, non-Chrome apps, the Chrome address bar/omnibox itself).

Keep the existing tools/chrome-tools/test-mcp-*.ps1 probes as a Windows fallback diagnostic only. They speak the raw 4-byte-LE-length-prefix pipe protocol directly to \\.\pipe\claude-mcp-browser-bridge-alton; use them to confirm the native host is alive if the MCP bridge reports 'Browser extension is not connected', not as the primary path.
capabilities: Acts inside the user's REAL logged-in Chrome session (shared cookies/login state) — reaches Gmail, Google Docs, Calendar, Notion, any already-authenticated site with zero API wiring or re-login; this is the single thing Playwright's isolated profile cannot do.

Vision-based interaction: the `computer` tool screenshots the tab viewport (Page.captureScreenshot) and clicks/types/scrolls by coordinate (Input.dispatchMouseEvent / Input.insertText / mouseWheel), so it handles canvas, custom widgets, and DOM-less UI where selectors fail.

`find` does natural-language element location against the page accessibility tree (returns up to ~20 matches with refs+coordinates), and `read_page` returns the accessibility tree directly — robust on messy/dynamic pages.

Less detectable by anti-automation: it drives a normal user Chrome via chrome.debugger rather than a --remote-debugging-port / navigator.webdriver Chromium, so CDP-fingerprinting and clean-profile anti-bot heuristics are less likely to trip.
Rich page-state reads: get_page_text, read_console_messages, read_network_requests, plus javascript_tool for in-page eval (via chrome.scripting.executeScript).
gif_creator records the interaction sequence (rrweb-based) to a shareable GIF — useful for documenting a flow; no Playwright/OS equivalent here.

Pauses and hands control back to the human on login pages and CAPTCHAs by design — a natural fit for the Chrome-autofill-first / Bitwarden-fallback credential model (the human or autofill completes the login, agent resumes).
Multi-tab / multi-site workflows via tabs_create_mcp within an MCP tab group distinct from the side-panel group.
file_upload / upload_image for file-picker and image-input flows.
limitations: VIEWPORT-ONLY: the `computer` tool cannot see or click the OS desktop, other monitors, the Chrome window chrome (address bar/omnibox, tab strip), or any non-Chrome app. It is not an OS controller. The 3-monitor full-desktop reach in the FIXED spec must come from the PowerShell/pyautogui layer, not this tool.

Coordinate space is SCALED viewport pixels, not raw screen pixels: screenshots are downscaled for token cost (pxPerToken 28, maxTargetPx 1568) and the extension keeps an internal scaling context so its own click coords map back. Coordinates are NOT interchangeable with the OS-layer's absolute 2560x1440 screen coordinates from click-at.ps1.

Cannot share a Chrome tab with a --remote-debugging-port CDP client: chrome.debugger allows only one attached debugger per tab ('Another debugger is already attached'). Chrome 136+ additionally ignores --remote-debugging-port on the default profile. The synced profile is therefore extension-only; Playwright must use its own separate profile/instance.

JavaScript dialogs (alert/confirm/prompt) BLOCK the page and freeze the bridge — the agent stops receiving commands until the dialog is dismissed manually. Documented hazard; design flows to avoid synchronous dialogs or expect a human dismiss.

Service-worker idle drop: the extension's MV3 service worker goes idle on long/quiet sessions, breaking the connection ('Receiving end does not exist'); requires /chrome reconnect. Not durable for unattended long-running automation without a keep-alive/reconnect loop.
Tab-ids are session-scoped and churn; reusing a stale tab-id yields 'No tab available'. Always re-derive from tabs_context_mcp.
Tools are DEFERRED — every mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tool must be ToolSearch-loaded before it is callable, or it errors with InputValidationError.

Windows bridge fragility: there is an open, unresolved Anthropic issue (#58220) where the MCP client and the native-host named pipe disagree on framing (client sends raw JSON-RPC, host expects 4-byte UInt32-LE length prefix), making every tool call return 'Browser extension is not connected' on some Windows builds. Also documented: named-pipe EADDRINUSE conflicts when multiple Claude Code sessions or Claude Desktop contend for the same pipe; the extension may bind to Claude Desktop instead of Claude Code if both are installed. Verify the bridge actually works on THIS Rocinante Claude Code build before depending on it.
Beta, Chrome/Edge only (no Brave/Arc, no WSL); requires a direct Anthropic plan (Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise), not third-party Bedrock/Vertex.
Headless-peer portability is zero for v1: rtxserver/gpuserver1 have no GUI Chrome and no extension host — this surface is Rocinante-only, as the FIXED scope already states.
integration_notes: Two browsers, not one. claude-in-chrome owns the DEDICATED synced profile via the extension; sartor-playwright keeps its existing isolated .playwright-profile Chromium (.mcp.json line 18). They never share a Chrome process, so the one-debugger-per-tab rule is moot — no contention to engineer around. Both run concurrently with Alton's daily Chrome (third profile). Router selection inside /computer-control: (1) if the task needs Alton's live logged-in session, or hits a site with anti-CDP/anti-bot defenses, or is a messy/canvas/vision page → claude-in-chrome. (2) if the task is deterministic DOM automation, a clean throwaway session, or needs PDF save / structured snapshot → sartor-playwright. (3) if the target is outside the browser viewport (other monitors, OS dialogs, the omnibox, non-Chrome apps, the Chrome password-manager UI at chrome://password-manager for the Bitwarden-fallback flow) → the PowerShell/pyautogui OS layer. Credential model fits cleanly: claude-in-chrome triggers Chrome autofill in the synced profile and pauses for login/CAPTCHA; when a site isn't saved, the OS layer drives chrome://password-manager or fetches via scripts/sartor-secret (bw), never printing the raw value. Note an explicit handoff seam: because claude-in-chrome's viewport coordinates are scaled and the OS layer's are absolute screen pixels, the router must NOT pass coordinates between the two surfaces — hand off by intent ('click the Save button'), each surface re-locates in its own space.
security_flags: FULL authenticated-session reach is the security center of gravity: claude-in-chrome operates inside Alton's real signed-in Google/Gmail/Docs/Calendar session. Any agent action there is performed AS Alton — directly implicates Constitution §7 (no sending under another's name without review). Gate all send/submit/post/delete actions in authenticated apps behind explicit human confirmation; the skill must not autonomously click 'Send' in Gmail or submit forms that transmit on Alton's behalf.

Children's-info and family-medical hard rules (§7) ride along: a synced, logged-in browser can surface kids' school portals, medical patient portals, etc. Reads/screenshots of such pages must not be externalized or logged. The viewport screenshot stream is sensitive PII by default.

Credential exposure surface: autofill and the chrome://password-manager fallback handle live secrets. Screenshots taken at the moment a password field is focused/revealed could capture secret values — never persist or transmit such frames; the 'raw secret values NEVER logged/printed' FIXED rule extends to screenshots and gif_creator recordings.
Constrain site permissions in the EXTENSION settings (Claude Code inherits them) to an allowlist; do not leave the synced profile open to act on every site, given full-session authority.

Prompt-injection via web content is a live risk for any vision/DOM agent acting in an authenticated session — a malicious page can attempt to steer the agent into privileged actions in Alton's logged-in tabs. Treat page-derived text/instructions as untrusted; do not let read_page/get_page_text content trigger consequential actions without confirmation.

gif_creator and javascript_tool can exfiltrate page state (in-page eval, DOM snapshots); ensure recordings/eval output of authenticated pages are not written to git-tracked or externally-mirrored paths.

Bridge mis-binding risk (issue #20887): if Claude Desktop is also installed, the extension may attach to it instead of Claude Code — meaning a different Claude process could be driving Alton's synced session than intended. Verify which client owns the pipe before granting autonomy.
open_questions: Does the claude-in-chrome MCP bridge actually function on THIS Rocinante Claude Code build, or does it hit the open Windows 4-byte-framing bug (#58220)? Must be live-tested before /computer-control depends on it; this research was read-only and did not exercise the bridge.
Service-worker idle drops break unattended runs — does /computer-control need an explicit reconnect/keep-alive wrapper for any long or scheduled browser task, or is it strictly interactive?

Chrome Sync ON pulls Alton's full saved-password set into the dedicated profile — is the agent's blast radius (acting in any synced-logged-in site) acceptable, or should the synced profile be scoped to a subset of accounts? This is a posture decision for Alton.

Exact coordinate transform between the extension's scaled viewport space and the OS layer's absolute screen pixels is not documented precisely (pxPerToken 28 / maxTargetPx 1568 are the known constants); confirm empirically if any cross-surface coordinate mapping is ever truly needed (recommendation is to avoid it).
Edge is supported and present-capable, but the spec targets Chrome — no action needed unless a profile-isolation problem pushes toward Edge as the dedicated-profile host.

surface: OS-level full-desktop control (Rocinante, Windows 10 Home 10.0.19045, 3 monitors)
recommended_approach: Extend the existing PowerShell .NET toolkit (option a) — do NOT add pyautogui or nut.js. Both third-party libs have documented, unresolved multi-monitor + mixed-DPI defects and both call SetProcessDpiAware() (system-DPI level, the wrong awareness for a 3-monitor mixed-scale rig) on import; adding either is a regression versus a corrected .NET layer that already lives in tools/chrome-tools/. The decisive win is that .NET P/Invoke gives direct access to the one mechanism that is provably correct across all three monitors including the negative-X left monitor: SendInput with MOUSEEVENTF_ABSOLUTE | MOUSEEVENTF_VIRTUALDESK and coordinates normalized to 0..65535 over the virtual desktop (SM_XVIRTUALSCREEN/SM_YVIRTUALSCREEN/SM_CXVIRTUALSCREEN/SM_CYVIRTUALSCREEN). The existing click-at.ps1 must be retired in favor of this: it uses SetCursorPos + legacy mouse_event in raw pixels with no virtual-desktop normalization and no DPI pinning, which is the actual bug surface. The corrected layer is ~4 small scripts (input.ps1, capture.ps1, a coordinate-mapping helper, and a safety-gate wrapper) sharing ONE coordinate convention. Keep Python only as an optional convenience caller; the privileged-input primitive stays in .NET. Net: lowest new dependency, highest coordinate fidelity, reuses code Alton already trusts, and stays portable in spirit (the see-then-click contract is OS-agnostic even though the implementation is Win32).
setup_steps: Pin DPI awareness FIRST in every OS-control entry point: call SetProcessDpiAwarenessContext(DPI_AWARENESS_CONTEXT_PER_MONITOR_AWARE_V2) (user32, Win10 1703+; -4 as the context value) at process start of both the capture script and the input script. This forces the process to see true physical pixels uniformly so the screenshot space == the click space. Without this the two scripts can silently live in different coordinate spaces and every click on a scaled monitor misses.

Create tools/os-control/coords.ps1 as the single source of truth for the virtual-desktop geometry: read GetSystemMetrics(SM_XVIRTUALSCREEN=76, SM_YVIRTUALSCREEN=77, SM_CXVIRTUALSCREEN=78, SM_CYVIRTUALSCREEN=79) at runtime instead of hardcoding 2560/1920 numbers. Emit a monitor manifest (per-monitor origin+size in physical px) so screenshots can be tagged with their absolute origin.

Rewrite the click engine as tools/os-control/input.ps1 using SendInput (NOT SetCursorPos/mouse_event). For a click at virtual-desktop physical pixel (Xv,Yv): normX = round((Xv - SM_XVIRTUALSCREEN) * 65535 / (SM_CXVIRTUALSCREEN - 1)); normY likewise; send one atomic INPUT array [MOVE absolute+virtualdesk, LEFTDOWN, LEFTUP]. Support flags: -Right, -Double, -Drag (down, N interpolated moves, up), -Wheel (MOUSEEVENTF_WHEEL, WHEEL_DELTA=120 per notch, signed). This is the only path that maps correctly onto the negative-X left monitor.

Rewrite capture as tools/os-control/capture.ps1: keep CopyFromScreen (it captures in physical pixels, which is correct once DPI is pinned) but drive its bounds from coords.ps1 at runtime, not the hardcoded table in the current screenshot.ps1. Add a -Cursor switch that draws a crosshair at the last-known cursor pos and a -Grid switch (overlay a labeled coordinate grid every 100px) for the model to self-calibrate. Default to per-monitor PNGs plus an optional full-virtual-desktop stitch.

Replace SendKeys-based typing for anything beyond ASCII: add a -Unicode path in input.ps1 using SendInput with KEYEVENTF_UNICODE (per-codepoint KEYDOWN/KEYUP, scan code = codepoint), which handles arbitrary text and avoids SendKeys' +^%~(){}[] escaping fragility. Keep a -Hotkey mode (e.g. ctrl+s, alt+tab) via virtual-key codes with KEYDOWN/KEYUP ordering for modifier chords.

Build tools/os-control/gate.ps1 as the MANDATORY wrapper every click/type/drag routes through: it enforces (1) corner-failsafe check, (2) deny-list region/window check, (3) confirm-before-click for destructive targets, (4) kill-switch flag-file check, before delegating to input.ps1. No agent path calls input.ps1 directly.

Author the /computer-control SKILL.md OS section: document the see-then-click loop, the coordinate contract (model reasons in screenshot pixels -> screenshot is tagged with monitor origin -> agent adds origin to get virtual-desktop physical px -> gate.ps1 -> SendInput normalizes to 0..65535), and the safety interlocks. Cross-link the existing chrome-automation SKILL.md so browser and OS share one skill.

Validation gate before first live use: a calibration self-test that clicks 9 known targets (a 3x3 grid) on EACH of the 3 monitors with -Grid overlay, screenshots after each, and confirms cursor landed within tolerance. Negative-X left monitor and the 2560x1440 primary are the two must-pass cases. Do this read-only/dry-run first (log intended coords without sending) per Constitution confirm-before-irreversible.
capabilities: Click/double/right-click/drag/scroll and type anywhere on any of the 3 monitors, any application — true full-desktop reach beyond the browser, which neither the Playwright MCP nor the claude-in-chrome MCP can do (they are DOM/tab-scoped).

Correct coordinate fidelity across a mixed-origin virtual desktop including the negative-X left monitor, via SendInput VIRTUALDESK normalization — the one mechanism the Win32 docs confirm maps across the entire virtual desktop rather than just the primary monitor.

Drives native OS surfaces the browser surfaces structurally cannot reach: Windows file-open/save dialogs, the Chrome 'Save password?' bubble (rendered as native browser chrome, outside the DOM), Windows credential prompts, UAC-adjacent app dialogs, print dialogs, taskbar, system tray.
Physical-pixel screenshots that share ONE coordinate space with the click engine once DPI awareness is pinned — the see-then-click loop closes without a hidden scaling transform.
Arbitrary Unicode text entry and modifier-chord hotkeys via SendInput KEYEVENTF_UNICODE / virtual-key codes, avoiding SendKeys escaping fragility.
Reuses code Alton already runs (the chrome-tools .NET scripts), so the trust surface and review burden are incremental, not new.
limitations: SendInput cannot interact with windows running at a higher integrity level than the agent process (UAC elevation prompts, the secure desktop, the Ctrl+Alt+Del / lock screen). Those are invisible to screenshot capture AND immune to synthetic input by design — the agent will appear to 'click nothing'. This is a hard OS boundary, not a bug to fix.

DPI awareness MUST be pinned to PER_MONITOR_AWARE_V2 in BOTH capture and input, set once at process start and never changed mid-process (DPI awareness is largely immutable after first UI call). If capture and input run in differently-DPI-aware processes, coordinates silently diverge and clicks miss on scaled monitors — the single highest-probability failure mode.

The current screenshot.ps1 hardcodes monitor geometry (2560x1440 @ 0,0; 1920x1080 @ 2560,159; 1920x1080 @ -1920,143). Any monitor rearrange, resolution change, or scaling change in Windows Display Settings invalidates those numbers. Must be replaced with runtime SM_*VIRTUALSCREEN reads or every click drifts.

SetCursorPos (current click-at.ps1) and pyautogui/nut.js mouse moves are DPI- and virtual-desktop-fragile; documented to be unreliable on secondary monitors and to break entirely under per-monitor mixed scaling. Do not keep them on the live path.

Screenshot capture is a point-in-time raster; the model can mis-identify a pixel target if UI moved between capture and click (animations, async loads, focus-steal popups). Mitigate with re-capture-after-action verification, not blind action chaining.

SendInput targets the FOREGROUND window / global cursor — there is no per-window targeting. A focus-steal popup (Windows Update, antivirus, a notification toast) that grabs foreground between see and click will receive the input. The deny-list + re-verify-after-capture is the only defense.
Windows 10 Home: no Group Policy, fewer lockdown levers than Pro; the safety model is entirely in our wrapper, not OS-enforced.

Headless Linux peers (rtxserver/gpuserver1) have no display — this entire surface is Rocinante-only for v1 as specified; portability of the see-then-click CONTRACT is free, but the Win32 implementation is not.
integration_notes: "This OS surface is the bottom tier of a three-tier control stack inside the single /computer-control skill, and the router should prefer the most-scoped tool that can do the job: (1) DOM-scoped browser MCP (claude-in-chrome / sartor-playwright) for in-page interaction — fastest, safest, semantic-selector-based, no pixel math; (2) the sartor-playwright vision cap (browser_mouse_click_xy etc.) for in-viewport pixel clicks when the DOM path fails but the target is inside the browser content area; (3) THIS OS layer only when the target is outside the browser DOM/viewport entirely. The clean composition story is the handoff: a browser action triggers a NATIVE surface the browser cannot script — file picker, 'Save password?' bubble, Windows credential prompt, print/save dialog — at which point control hands off to OS capture+SendInput, completes the native interaction, then hands back to the browser MCP. The Chrome dedicated profile (Sync ON) means most logins autofill before any native prompt appears, so the OS layer mostly handles file dialogs and the password-save bubble rather than credential entry; Bitwarden fallback (scripts/sartor-secret) feeds text into native fields via the input.ps1 -Unicode path WITHOUT the secret ever being logged. Both Chrome instances (Alton's daily + the dedicated automation profile) coexist; the OS layer must use window-handle/title checks (as focus-chrome.ps1 already does) to ensure it acts on the automation window, never Alton's personal session. Router rule of thumb: stay in tier 1/2 whenever the target is reachable in-DOM; drop to tier 3 only on explicit native-surface need, and route every tier-3 action through gate.ps1."
security_flags: HIGHEST-RISK SURFACE in the whole skill: full synthetic input on Alton's PRIMARY workstation means the agent can click/type into ANY app — banking, brokerage (Chase/portfolio per Domains 1&4), email, irreversible OS actions — with no DOM-level guardrail. Constitution §7 hard constraints (no autonomous money movement, no sending under another's name) are NOT structurally enforced by the OS; they must be enforced by gate.ps1's deny-list + confirm gate. Treat any pixel that could be inside a financial app, a 'Send'/'Submit'/'Pay'/'Transfer'/'Confirm' button, or a system dialog as confirm-required by default.

FAILSAFE (corner-abort): implement a pyautogui-style failsafe in gate.ps1 — before any send, read GetCursorPos; if Alton has slammed the cursor into any screen corner of the virtual desktop, abort the whole action sequence and raise. Because SendInput moves the cursor, the agent must NOT itself trigger this; check the cursor position at the START of a human-interruptible idle window, and additionally honor a physical-corner park as an explicit 'hands off' signal. This is the human's instant veto.

KILL SWITCH: a watched flag-file (e.g. tools/os-control/STOP) and a global hotkey hook; gate.ps1 checks the flag before every primitive and aborts immediately if present. Document one keystroke Alton can hit to drop the flag. Kill switch must also halt any in-progress drag (release the button) so a half-finished drag can't leave a window mid-move.

CONFIRM-BEFORE-CLICK on destructive/irreversible targets: gate.ps1 requires explicit confirmation when the target region matches a destructive-intent classifier — buttons reading Delete/Remove/Send/Pay/Transfer/Buy/Sell/Confirm/Format/Shut down, OR any window whose title/class matches a financial/email/system app. Default-deny for ambiguous targets; the cost of asking is lower than the cost of a mis-click that moves money or sends mail (Constitution fail-safe doctrine).

DENY-LIST of regions/apps the agent must NEVER click without explicit per-action confirm: the Windows taskbar + system tray (bottom strip of primary), the Start menu, any window with class #32770 that is a UAC/security dialog, the lock screen, and by title: Chase, brokerage, Bitwarden desktop, Stripe, IRS/banking tabs, and Alton's PERSONAL (non-automation) Chrome window. Maintain as data (regex on window title/class + screen-rect zones) so it's auditable.

SECRET HANDLING: Bitwarden-sourced credentials typed via SendInput KEYEVENTF_UNICODE must never be echoed to stdout, the action log, or screenshots. Audit log records 'typed N chars into <window> field' — never the value. Screenshots taken while a password field has focus should be skipped or the field region redacted, because a clear-text reveal toggle could expose the secret into a logged image (Constitution §7 family-medical / credential externalization discipline applies by analogy).

FOCUS-STEAL HIJACK: synthetic input goes to whatever holds foreground at send time. A malicious or merely badly-timed popup (fake dialog, notification, another app grabbing focus) can capture a click or keystroke meant elsewhere — including capturing a typed credential. Mandatory mitigation: re-capture-and-verify foreground window identity immediately before sending, and abort if foreground changed since the screenshot the decision was based on.

AUDIT TRAIL: every OS primitive (intended coords, normalized coords, resolved monitor, target window title/class, action type, gate decision) appends to an audit log, mirroring the gpu-business-ops 'log all marketplace interactions' discipline. This is the only forensic record for a surface with no DOM and no app-level log.

DRY-RUN DEFAULT for first use and after any monitor/scaling change: gate.ps1 supports a -WhatIf mode that logs intended coordinates and resolved targets WITHOUT sending input, so the calibration grid can be validated before any live click — satisfies confirm-before-irreversible during bring-up.
open_questions: DPI scaling per monitor is unverified in this read-only research (no probing allowed). If the primary 2560x1440 or either 1080p side monitor runs at non-100% scaling, the PER_MONITOR_AWARE_V2 pin is load-bearing and the hardcoded geometry in screenshot.ps1 is already wrong. First live step must read GetDpiForMonitor on all three before trusting any coordinate.

Does the agent's host process (Claude Code shell) run elevated? If not, it cannot drive UAC prompts or any elevated app window — need to confirm whether that's an acceptable v1 limitation or whether a (carefully gated) elevated helper is warranted.

Exact deny-list window-title/class set for the financial and personal-Chrome windows needs Alton's input — the classifier is only as safe as that list, and getting it wrong in the permissive direction is a Constitution §7 exposure.

Global-hotkey kill switch vs flag-file: a low-level keyboard hook is the most responsive kill switch but adds a background process; confirm whether the flag-file + corner-failsafe pair is sufficient for Alton or whether the hook is wanted.

Whether to physically park the automation work on a specific monitor (e.g. the left negative-X monitor) so Alton's primary 2560x1440 stays human-owned — would shrink the blast radius and simplify the deny-list, at the cost of forcing target windows onto one display.

surface: PASSWORD/CREDENTIAL surface for /computer-control — Chrome password-manager autofill (primary) + Bitwarden fallback, zero raw-secret leakage
recommended_approach: Drive login through a dedicated, Chrome-Sync-signed-in profile and let Chrome's own password manager do the filling — but trigger it with TRUSTED input events, not DOM value-sets. The autofill dropdown is a native (non-DOM) overlay, so the agent must (a) focus the username field with a real mouse click (CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent / browser_mouse_click_xy, which emit isTrusted=true), (b) send a real keystroke or ArrowDown via CDP Input.dispatchKeyEvent / browser_press_key to surface the dropdown, then (c) ArrowDown→Enter to accept the top saved credential — purely keyboard, so no raw value ever transits the agent. Playwright's page.fill / form_input set element.value directly and do NOT fire Chrome autofill; they are the wrong tool for the primary path. The mandatory precondition is disabling the per-profile "Use Windows Hello when filling passwords" toggle (and the chrome://flags biometric-reauth flag) on the isolated profile, because Windows Hello reauth gates filling itself, not just viewing — and an unattended agent cannot satisfy a biometric prompt. This beats DPAPI-decrypt (explicitly out of scope) and beats typing-everything-from-Bitwarden because it keeps the secret inside Chrome's process where it already lives synced. Bitwarden is the fallback for sites Chrome has no entry for: fetch BY NAME via sartor-secret, push into the field through the clipboard-paste pattern (PowerShell Set-Clipboard from a never-displayed variable → Ctrl+V keystroke → immediate clipboard clobber), never through a tool-call argument. Router order: try Chrome autofill, detect fill, on miss fall to Bitwarden, on miss/lock ask Alton.
setup_steps: ONE-TIME, ALTON AT KEYBOARD: Launch the dedicated profile interactively (not headless) — chrome.exe --user-data-dir=C:\Users\alto8\chrome-automation-profile (the existing automation profile dir, or a new dedicated one). Do NOT reuse Alton's daily profile; both run concurrently with separate --user-data-dir.

ONE-TIME, ALTON AT KEYBOARD: In that profile click the avatar → 'Turn on sync' → sign into Alton's Google account → 'Yes, I'm in' (choose 'Keep my data separate' if prompted). This is the interactive sign-in that must be a human; OAuth + any 2FA cannot be automated and should not be.

ONE-TIME: Confirm passwords sync down — visit chrome://password-manager/passwords and verify entries are present (Settings → 'Manage what you sync' must have Passwords ON; default is Sync Everything).

ONE-TIME, CRITICAL GATE: In the dedicated profile go to chrome://password-manager/settings and turn OFF 'Use Windows Hello when filling passwords'. Also set chrome://flags → search 'biometric' → 'Biometric authentication reauth before filling' = Disabled, relaunch. Without this, every autofill (and every view of a saved password) raises a native Windows Hello/Windows-Security dialog the agent cannot answer. Acceptable because the profile is isolated from Alton's daily browsing.

VERIFY CHROME 136+ CONSTRAINT: --remote-debugging-port is ignored on the DEFAULT data dir as of Chrome 136 (running 148 here). The dedicated --user-data-dir already satisfies this; CDP will attach to the synced profile with passwords present. Launch with --remote-debugging-port=9223 --remote-allow-origins=* --user-data-dir=<dedicated> (the existing chrome-automation skill pattern, but pointed at the SYNCED dir instead of a throwaway temp dir).

VERIFY the dedicated profile path is wired into whichever browser surface the skill uses (the existing sartor-playwright MCP currently points at .playwright-profile — for autofill it must point at the synced dedicated profile, OR use claude-in-chrome / CDP against the synced profile on 9223).

BITWARDEN PREREQ: confirm sartor-secret status returns 'unlocked' before any fallback attempt; if locked, do NOT prompt — surface to Alton. Add scripts dir to PATH per secrets-via-bitwarden skill (already done on Rocinante).
Encode the router decision logic (see integration_notes) into the skill body: autofill-first, detect, Bitwarden-fallback, ask-Alton on double-miss or lock.
capabilities: Logs into any site whose credentials are saved in Chrome's password manager WITHOUT the secret ever entering the agent's context — the value flows Chrome-internal, agent only sends focus-click + ArrowDown + Enter keystrokes.
Chrome Sync keeps the saved-password set fresh automatically: anything Alton saves in his daily Chrome appears in the dedicated profile within sync latency, so coverage grows without skill changes.

chrome://password-manager/passwords UI is itself a normal web surface (WebUI) — once the biometric gate is disabled, the agent can view/add/edit/delete saved credentials via DOM (search box, add button, fields) for password MANAGEMENT, not just use.
Bitwarden fallback (sartor-secret read '<NAME>') covers the long tail Chrome hasn't saved, fetching by name with locked-vault handling already built into the wrapper (exit 2 = locked, halt).

Full-desktop control means the autofill dropdown — a native non-DOM overlay — can be driven either by in-page keyboard (preferred) OR, if needed, by OS-level pixel click via the existing click-at.ps1 / screenshot.ps1 multi-monitor toolkit.
Clipboard-paste pattern (already proven in Playbook 2 of secrets-via-bitwarden) types Bitwarden secrets into fields with the value never appearing in any tool-call argument or transcript.
limitations: Chrome autofill does NOT fire on DOM value-injection. Playwright page.fill() / browser_fill_form / claude-in-chrome form_input set element.value directly and bypass the autofill engine — using them for the primary path silently breaks autofill. Must use trusted input (real mouse click to focus + real keystroke), which only CDP Input.dispatch* / browser_mouse_click_xy / browser_press_key / OS-level click provide.

The native autofill dropdown is NOT in the DOM or the accessibility tree — browser_snapshot/read_page/find cannot see or click it. It must be navigated by keyboard (ArrowDown→Enter) or by OS pixel-click on the popup. No DOM selector exists for the credential row.

Windows Hello / biometric reauth gates BOTH filling and viewing saved passwords when enabled — a hard blocker for an unattended agent. The setup step disables it on the isolated profile; if Alton ever wants it back on, the autofill path requires live OS-level Windows Hello handling (PIN/biometric), which is not automatable without his presence.

One-time Google sign-in (and any 2FA) is irreducibly interactive — Alton must do it once at the keyboard. Token/cookie expiry could force a re-sign-in later; the skill must detect 'signed out of sync' and ask, not retry.
Chrome 136+ refuses --remote-debugging-port on the default user-data-dir; only works because we use a dedicated dir. A future regression that points CDP at the default profile would break silently.

Autofill matches by origin (scheme+eTLD+1). Subdomain/alias mismatches, embedded login iframes from a different origin, and shadow-DOM login widgets can suppress the dropdown even when a credential exists — these fall through to Bitwarden.

Two password managers in one profile (Chrome's + any Bitwarden browser extension) can race and produce competing dropdowns; the dedicated profile should NOT have the Bitwarden extension installed — Bitwarden fallback goes through the CLI/clipboard, not an extension.

Sync-down is not instantaneous; a password Alton saved seconds ago may not yet be in the dedicated profile — treat 'no autofill' as possibly-just-stale and fall to Bitwarden rather than concluding the credential doesn't exist.
integration_notes: "Router decision logic (autofill vs Bitwarden vs ask-Alton): (1) Navigate to login page in the synced dedicated profile. (2) Locate the username/identifier field (DOM find is fine for LOCATING; only the FILL must be trusted-input). (3) Real-mouse-click the field to focus, send one trusted keystroke or ArrowDown to surface the native dropdown. (4) DETECT autofill: after ArrowDown→Enter, read the field's value-length / 'is non-empty' via JS (length only, never the value) or check the password field became masked-populated. If populated → submit, done. (5) MISS (field still empty after the keyboard dance, OR origin has no saved entry): fall to Bitwarden — sartor-secret status; if unlocked, sartor-secret read '<site NAME>' --field username / password, push via the PowerShell clipboard→Ctrl+V→clobber pattern (never via form_input value=). (6) Bitwarden returns exit 3 (not-found) OR exit 2 (locked): STOP and ask Alton — locked vault is never self-unlocked, unknown credential is never guessed. Concurrency: the synced dedicated profile runs alongside Alton's daily Chrome (separate --user-data-dir, separate process) — both fine. Peers (rtxserver/gpuserver1) are headless Linux: Chrome-Sync autofill is non-portable there (no interactive Google sign-in, no display), but the Bitwarden fallback branch IS portable verbatim — note this so the skill's Bitwarden path stays OS-agnostic for a future headless port."
security_flags: RAW-SECRET-IN-TOOL-ARG: The dominant leak vector. NEVER use form_input value=<secret>, browser_type text=<secret>, browser_fill_form, or page.fill with a real credential — those serialize the value into the tool-call args which land in the transcript/.jsonl. Bitwarden values go ONLY via clipboard-paste (Set-Clipboard from a never-printed PowerShell var → Ctrl+V keystroke). Chrome autofill values never leave Chrome at all.

STDOUT-CAPTURE LEAK: sartor-secret read prints the value to stdout (by design, no trailing newline). If the agent runs it via the Bash/PowerShell tool and the command's stdout is captured into the transcript, the secret leaks. Mitigation: consume the value inside a single PowerShell scope that pipes straight to Set-Clipboard and prints NOTHING ($pw = & sartor-secret.cmd read 'X'; Set-Clipboard $pw; the tool output must be empty). Never run `sartor-secret read X` as a bare command whose output the harness echoes.

CLIPBOARD RESIDUE: The clipboard is a per-session global readable by any window (including Alton's daily Chrome and any clipboard-history feature). Must clobber immediately after paste (Set-Clipboard -Value ' ') and Remove-Variable + [GC]::Collect(). Windows 10 Clipboard History (Win+V) can retain entries — verify it's off on Rocinante or the pasted secret persists in history.

WINDOWS-HELLO-DISABLED widens exposure: turning off the biometric fill-gate on the dedicated profile means anyone with access to that profile (or full-desktop control of Rocinante) can read saved passwords via chrome://password-manager with no reauth. Acceptable only because the profile is dedicated/isolated and Rocinante access already implies trust — but it is a real downgrade; document it and keep the profile out of any shared/remote-desktop exposure.

SYNCED-PROFILE = ALTON'S FULL PASSWORD VAULT on disk: signing the automation profile into Alton's Google account pulls his entire saved-password set into a profile an autonomous agent drives. The agent must never be asked (by a non-principal — cf. 'kid prompts are not principal') to exfiltrate, screenshot, or read-aloud chrome://password-manager entries. Add a guard: viewing/exporting the password list is a management action that surfaces to Alton, not a casual capability.

SCREENSHOT LEAK: Full-desktop screenshot/region tools may capture a momentarily-visible password field (some sites show a 'reveal' eye toggle) or the autofill dropdown text. Never screenshot during/after a fill of a reveal-enabled field; if a screenshot is needed for navigation, take it before fill or with the password field masked.

OS-CLICK ON NATIVE POPUP via pixel coordinates is brittle and could mis-click onto an adjacent window on a 3-monitor desktop, potentially submitting a form with wrong/partial data or clicking an unintended app — prefer keyboard navigation of the dropdown over pixel-clicking it.

CONSTITUTION §7: this surface can autofill into login forms but must never AUTONOMOUSLY move money or send under another identity once logged in — being logged into Chase/Stripe/email via autofill does not grant transaction/send authority; those remain confirm-before-act.
open_questions: Does the existing sartor-playwright MCP (--user-data-dir .playwright-profile) need to be repointed at the synced dedicated profile, or should the skill use claude-in-chrome / raw-CDP-on-9223 against the synced profile instead? Pick ONE browser surface that owns the synced profile to avoid two engines fighting over the same user-data-dir (Chrome locks a profile to one running instance).

Is Windows 10 Clipboard History (Win+V) enabled on Rocinante? If yes, the Bitwarden clipboard-paste fallback leaves secrets in history and must be disabled or the fallback redesigned (e.g., SendKeys char-by-char from a variable instead of clipboard).
Confirm the dedicated profile must NOT have a Bitwarden browser extension installed (to avoid dueling autofill dropdowns) — current automation profile contents unknown; verify.

What is the detection signal for 'autofill fired'? Reading field.value to confirm leaks the value; need a length-only / masked-state probe — validate that JS `document.querySelector(sel).value.length>0` is reliable across sites and never logs the value.
Sync-token longevity: how often will the dedicated profile get signed out of Google (forcing Alton to re-auth)? Determines whether the skill needs a periodic 'sync still alive?' health check.
